sections.paragraph:
συμβούλιον, ου, τό [σύν, βούλομαι]

—1. ‘engagement in deliberation by a group’, w. blend (as determined by syntactical connection esp. in conjunction w. such verbs as λαμβάνειν, διδόναι, ποιεῖν, ἑτοιμάζειν) of counsel in deliberative sense and the product of such deliberation decision, plan. Engl. idiom may reproduce the formulaic σ. λαμβάνειν w. such rendering as reach a decision, form a plan, or simply decide.

—a. in general deliberative sense σ. ποιεῖν/ἑτοιμάζειν reach a decision Mk 15:1; σ. λαμβ. ἀγοράζειν decide to purchase Mt 27:7; σ. λαμβ. διδόναι decide to give 28:12.

—b. w. focus on intentionality: in hostile sense (σ. λαμβάνειν/διδόναι, which can be rendered either as form a plot or simply w. the verb plot) w. κατά Mt 12:14; 27:1; Mk 3:6; w. ὅπως form a plot Mt 22:15.

—2. ‘deliberative or advisory body’, council Ac 25:12.
